articles.title: Arsenal fans are still upbeat
articles.description: Arsenal fans reacted outside the Emirates Stadium after seeing their side lose on penalties to PSG in the Champions League final.

articles.title: A big dilemma for AFTV's Robbie
articles.description: Arsenal Fan TV's Robbie Lyle says he would give up all his online followers to see his club win the Champions League against Paris Saint-Germain on Saturday.

articles.description: Police in Budapest have made arrests and are studying CCTV footage to identify supporters fighting before Saturday's Champions League final between Arsenal and Paris St-Germain.

articles.description: Aaron Ramsey describes Mikel Arteta as an inspiration as he aims to follow his former Arsenal team-mate's success.

articles.description: BBC Sport's tactics correspondent Umir Irfan looks at how Arsenal can defeat Paris St-Germain in Saturday's Champions League final.

articles.title: Arsenal's Timber fit to start Champions League final
articles.description: Mikel Arteta says that Dutch defender Jurrien Timber is fit to start the Champions League final after more than two months out with a groin injury.

articles.title: Saka, Odegaard and messages from legends
articles.description: Club legends Thierry Henry and Patrick Viera have wished Arsenal players Bukayo Saka and Martin Odegaard good luck ahead of the Champions League final.

articles.description: Declan Rice shares what winning the double would mean to Arsenal ahead of the Champions League final.

articles.description: Fans will need a subscription to watch the Champions League final between Arsenal and PSG after TNT Sports decides against making the match free to air.

articles.description: Twelve years ago Arsenal keeper David Raya made his debut for non-league Southport; on Saturday he plays in the Champions League final - this is how he got there.

articles.title: Covid era gave Arteta space to revive Arsenal, says Kroenke
articles.description: Arsenal co-chair Josh Kroenke says behind-closed-doors football during the Covid-19 pandemic allowed manager Mikel Arteta "space" that helped to revive the "sleeping giant".

articles.title: Meet the artist behind these murals
articles.description: BBC Sport speaks to Northbanksy, an anonymous street artist that has been producing Arsenal-theme works around North London since 2019.

articles.title: New York mayor wears Arsenal kurta during Eid prayers
articles.description: The mayor of New York City Zohran Mamdani takes his Arsenal super-fandom up a notch by attending Eid al-Adha prayers in the Bronx wearing an Arsenal-themed tunic.

articles.description: Why Paris St-Germain will be going into Saturday's Champions League final against Arsenal with one advantage.

articles.description: BBC Sport assesses how VAR interventions may have impacted games at key moments and how some teams might appreciate the system more than others.

articles.title: ‘Happy either way’: Arsenal fans find zen attitude to Champions League final
articles.description: Supporters filling north London pubs said they were already gratified by Premier League win The streets of Holloway, usually bustling with families and trolly-dragging shoppers, were uncharacteristically quiet on Saturday afternoon. articles.title: Cruel ending to Arsenal’s thrillingly intense, high-quality evening | Barney Ronay
articles.description: Some struggle to love Arteta’s side but they went toe-to-toe with Paris Saint-Germain to produce a memorable final It always seemed likely, somehow, that Arsenal’s season was going to come down to Gabriel Magalhães and a set piece. articles.title: Arteta urges Arsenal to use Champions League final pain against PSG and ‘turn it into fuel’
articles.description: ‘We will have to improve to try to get a different outcome’ Manager unhappy Arsenal not given extra-time penalty Mikel Arteta spoke of his heartache after a skied penalty from Gabriel Magalhães in the shootout against Paris Saint-Germain ended Arsenal’s hopes of being crowned European champions for the first time, but emphasised the need to take that pain “and turn it into fuel”. articles.title: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal: PSG win Champions League final on penalties – as it happened

articles.description: Désiré Doué inspired PSG’s fightback to win in a shootout after being stunned by early goal from a classy Kai Havertz Matvey Saf onov Appeared to forget that goalkeepers are allowed to use their hands for Havertz’s strike. articles.title: Paris Saint-Germain retain Champions League as Arsenal dream dashed in shootout
articles.description: It was a showpiece that held the football world in its grip, the tension mounting exponentially, everything on the line. For Paris Saint-Germain, there was the opportunity to make it clear that this is a dynastic team; the rarity of retaining a Champions League title. For Arsenal, it was simple. Never mind the Invincibles. They stood to be immortal, a first victory in this competition to follow th

articles.title: When Arsenal beat PSG on their way to winning the Cup Winners’ Cup
articles.description: Arsenal relied on their brilliant defence to see off PSG in the semi-finals and frustrate Parma in the final in 1994 By That 1980s Sports Blog The Cup Winners’ Cup became a victim of the Champions League’s expansion in the late 1990s, but there could be no questioning the quality of the competition when Arsenal won it in the 1993-94 season. articles.title: Arsenal fans use ‘trains, planes and automobiles’ in race to Budapest for Champions League final
articles.description: As flights and hotels sell out and prices soar, supporters go to great lengths to see Gunners face Paris Saint-Germain Since Arsenal’s euphoric Premier League win last week, it has been a never-ending party in much of north London (apart from in Tottenham, although they had their own cause for celebration ). articles.title: More than 150 million people will watch tonight’s Champions League final. It’s PSG v Arsenal – and most of Africa | Sean Jacobs
articles.description: Many teams have fans abroad, but the bond between the north London club and ordinary Africans is on a different level. A continent expects If Arsenal win the Champions League final later today, expect euphoria across Africa. articles.title: My Arsenal devotion began with watching them lose in a South African cinema
articles.description: As a boy in the apartheid era I saw footage of the Gunners beaten in the 1969 League Cup final – on Saturday I will attend the Champions League showpiece with my son I fell for Arsenal in the white‑and-black world of apartheid, where television was banned as a tool of communist propaganda and the club of my dreams was 6,000 miles away and mostly invisible to me. articles.title: Liberation of Premier League title can help Arsenal blunt PSG and join Europe’s elite
articles.description: Mikel Arteta must find the balance between newfound freedom and tried-and-tested solidity against Luis Enrique’s attacking machine Welcome to Budapest: city of stew, city of pavement squares, city of men in cotton smocks offering brisk muscular relaxation in geothermally heated cubicles. articles.title: Arteta insists Arsenal’s ‘ambition is bigger’ for Champions League glory after title win
articles.description: Gunners face PSG in Budapest final on Saturday ‘We have one, and now we want the second one’ Mikel Arteta has dismissed suggestions the pressure is off Arsenal in Saturday’s Champions League final after their first Premier League title for 22 years and insists he and his players are hungry for more trophies. articles.title: Should Keir Starmer follow Mikel Arteta’s leadership method? | Letters
articles.description: Readers respond to Jonathan Freedland’s article on Arsenal and one of its most famous fans Congratulations to Arsenal on winning the Premier League. articles.description: Luis Enrique: ‘Retaining it is source of inspiration for us’ Expects to name same outfield 10 as in 2025 final Luis Enrique has insisted Paris Saint-Germain’s motivation to retain their Champions League title is greater than Arsenal’s quest to be crowned European champions for the first time. PSG demolished Inter 5-0 in last year’s final in Munich and are strong favourites for Saturday’s showdown

articles.description: In the runup to the Champions League final, the Arsenal manager’s first footballing steps in the Basque Country and Barcelona are recalled by those who shared them The way Santi Cazorla tells it, rolling about laughing, Mikel Arteta may just be the worst person you could ever wish to watch a match with. articles.title: Mamdani made a play for fashion’s premier league in his custom-made Arsenal kurta
articles.description: The New York mayor scored a range of responses attending Eid prayers in an outfit combining football and faith Since Arsenal won the Premier League for the first time in 22 years this month, the visibility of the club’s shirts has soared, with celebrities including Romeo Beckham and the singer Mahalia wearing them. articles.title: ‘Everything just clicked right there’: the moment that made Bukayo Saka
articles.description: Arsenal’s former head of coaching recalls the words from Freddie Ljungberg that shaped the young winger’s career Jan van Loon remembers the moment well. As Arsenal’s head of coaching he was guiding Freddie Ljungberg’s first steps in management with the under-15s when Bukayo Saka joined the squad. articles.title: Fresh v fatigued? Why PSG have a big advantage in Champions League final
articles.description: Luis Enrique rotated his squad in Ligue 1, whereas Mikel Arteta relied on his best XI in three domestic competitions By Opta Analyst A look at the most basic numbers might have you believe that the Champions League finalists have had equally demanding campaigns. articles.title: Kai Havertz: ‘Just to watch the Champions League final is very special, to play in it is unreal’
articles.description: Arsenal striker scored the winner in the final five years ago and is determined to make up for being ‘in a bad place’ when injured this season When Kai Havertz thinks back to the 2021 Champions League final, he can’t help smiling. articles.title: Arsenal owners promise to strengthen squad even if Champions League is won
articles.description: Josh Kroenke says there will be no ‘standing still’ Extending Mikel Arteta’s contract the ‘utmost priority’ Josh Kroenke has promised that ­Arsenal will strengthen their squad even if they are crowned European champions for the first time and said rewarding Mikel Arteta with a new contract is an “utmost priority”. Arsenal, who face Paris Saint-­Germain in the Champions League final in Budapest on

articles.title: Arsenal suffer penalty heartbreak as PSG win Champions League final
articles.description: Arsenal suffered heartbreak in the Champions League final in Budapest as they were beaten 4-3 on penalties by PSG after a tense 1-1 draw in 120 minutes.

articles.title: Defending champion Gauff suffers shock third-round exit at French Open
articles.description: Defending French ‌Open champion Coco Gauff ​suffered a shock third-round exit at Roland-Garros as the world No 4 was beaten 4-6 ​7-6 (7-1) 6-4 by Anastasia Potapova.

articles.title: Youthful Wigan surpass St Helens in clear power shift – 'Change is coming at Saints'
articles.description: For four years in a row between 2021 and 2024, St Helens experienced Women's Challenge Cup glory. In the two years since, though, Wigan Warriors have dispatched Saints in two Wembley finals.

articles.title: Wigan cruise to Challenge Cup win over Hull KR
articles.description: Wigan Warriors scored seven tries as they cruised to a 40-10 victory over Hull KR in the Challenge Cup final at Wembley Stadium.

articles.description: Highlights from the second T20 between England and India in Bristol.

articles.title: Osaka sparkles again to set up Sabalenka clash at French Open
articles.description: Naomi Osaka once again lit up Roland-Garros with her choice of outfit as well as her talent on court as she beat American teenager Iva Jovic to set up a meeting with world No 1 Aryna Sabalenka in the fourth round of the French Open.

articles.title: Ukraine's Oliynykova condemns 'evil' war after loss to Russia's Shnaider
articles.description: Oleksandra Oliynykova gave an impassioned plea for tennis to stand up against the "evil" war in Ukraine after losing to Russia's Diana Shnaider in the third round of the French Open.

articles.description: Scotland midfielder Billy Gilmour has been ruled out of the World Cup after suffering a knee injury in the win over Curacao.

articles.description: Gabriel missed Arsenal's fifth penalty as PSG celebrated winning the Champions League in successive years.

articles.title: How does Iraola's style of play compare to Klopp and Slot?
articles.description: With Andoni Iraola being linked with the Liverpool job, Sky Sports News' Mark McAdam analyses how his style of play compares to Jurgen Klopp and Arne Slot.

articles.title: Peet tells Wigan to enjoy Challenge Cup celebrations - 'go as hard as you can'
articles.description: After clinching the Challenge Cup with a 40-10 drubbing of Hull KR, Wigan Warriors head coach Matt Peet had one message for his side on how to celebrate: "Go as hard as you can for as long as you can".

articles.title: Clarke 'praying' Gilmour makes World Cup
articles.description: Scotland boss Steve Clarke says he is "100 per cent worried" about Billy Gilmour after the midfielder hobbled off injured against Curacao on the eve of the World Cup.

articles.title: Dominant Wigan thrash St Helens in Women's Challenge Cup final
articles.description: Wigan Warriors further established themselves as the dominant force in women's rugby league with a 54-6 thrashing of St Helens in Saturday's Challenge Cup final at Wembley.

articles.title: Redknapp: Player power contributed to Slot exit, but Iraola can handle pressure
articles.description: Jamie Redknapp has suggested player power had a role in Arne Slot's sacking by Liverpool, but has backed Andoni Iraola to handle the pressure of replacing the Dutchman.

articles.title: French Open scores: Tiafoe and Auger-Aliassime in action
Scotland beat 10-man Curacao 4-1 in World Cup send-off match
articles.description: A heavily-rotated Scotland eventually eased past 10-man Curacao 4-1 in their final match on home soil before heading off to the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Johnston falls back in Austria as Kaneko leads heading into final round
articles.description: Andrew 'Beef' Johnston fell back after starting the day atop the leaderboard at the Austrian Alpine Open and sits four shots off the pace set by Japan's Kota Kaneko heading into Sunday's final round.
